name: Ginger-soy rice bowl
subtitle: with crispy egg & wilted spinach
summary: A 22-minute weeknight bowl that makes pantry rice and a jammy egg feel like a plan. Toasted sesame, soft-wilted spinach, and a ginger-soy glaze that turns sticky as it hits the hot pan.

steps.d: Rinse 1 cup of rice until water runs mostly clear. Cook with 1 1/4 cups water, covered, 18 minutes. Rest 5 minutes off heat.
Grate a thumb of ginger. Whisk with 3 tbsp soy, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sugar, and a splash of sesame oil.
Heat 1 tbsp neutral oil over medium-high. Crack eggs in; spoon hot oil over the whites until edges lace and yolks stay jammy, about 90 seconds.
Off heat, dump spinach into the still-hot pan. Toss with tongs until just collapsed.
Rice in a shallow bowl. Spinach on top. Eggs alongside. Spoon glaze over the whole thing; finish with scallions and sesame seeds.

name: Tomato-butter spaghetti
subtitle: marcella-style, 35 minutes, total comfort
summary: Three ingredients do most of the work: butter, onion, canned tomatoes. It tastes like a restaurant you keep meaning to go back to.

steps.d: Empty the can of tomatoes into a wide pot. Add butter and the halved onion, cut-side down. Simmer, uncovered, 35 minutes, breaking up tomatoes with a spoon now and then.
In the last 10 minutes, bring a big salted pot of water to a boil. Cook spaghetti 1 minute shy of package time.
Pluck the onion out of the sauce (save it, snack). Add cooked pasta plus a splash of pasta water; toss over low heat until sauce clings.
Shallow bowls, heavy Parmesan, a grind of black pepper.

name: Sheet-pan harissa chicken
subtitle: with chickpeas & charred lemon
summary: One pan, big flavor. Harissa catches on the skin; chickpeas crisp in the chicken fat.

steps.d: Pat thighs dry. Toss with harissa, olive oil, salt.
Chicken skin-up on a sheet pan with drained chickpeas and a halved lemon. 425 F, 38-42 min until skin is dark and crackly.
Rest 5 min. Squeeze the roasted lemon over everything. Cilantro on top.

name: Miso-glazed salmon
subtitle: with sticky rice & cucumber
summary: A 5-minute broil. The glaze does everything.

steps.d: Whisk miso, mirin, brown sugar, a splash of soy.
Brush on salmon. Broil on high 5-6 minutes until lacquered.
Rice, salmon, quick-sliced cucumber with rice vinegar and salt.

name: Crispy tofu larb
subtitle: with herbs, lime, and toasted rice
summary: The toasted rice is non-negotiable. It changes the whole thing.

steps.d: Dry-toast rice in a pan until deep gold. Grind to coarse powder.
Crumble tofu. Pan-fry in oil until edges are brown and crunchy, 8 min.
Off heat, toss with shallots, herbs, lime juice, fish sauce, chili, toasted rice powder.
On lettuce leaves or over rice.

name: Butter chicken
subtitle: the family's version, mild, tomato-forward
summary: The version Maya actually eats. Less heat, more cream, charred tomatoes.

steps.d: Brown chicken pieces in butter. Set aside.
Sweat onion + ginger + garlic. Add garam masala, tomatoes; simmer 15 min.
Return chicken, cream. Simmer 15 more min. Finish with cilantro.

name: Roasted carrot soup
subtitle: with brown butter & sage
summary: Sweet carrots, nutty brown butter, green sage oil. Fall-forward.

steps.d: Toss carrots + onion in oil. Roast at 425 F, 28 min.
Blend with stock until silky.
Melt butter, cook until nutty. Fry sage 15 sec. Drizzle over soup.

name: Chickpea-chard stew
subtitle: tomato-y, lemony, done in 25
summary: Chickpeas get creamy. Chard turns silky. Lemon at the end wakes it up.

steps.d: Thin-slice garlic; sizzle in olive oil until pale gold.
Add tomatoes, chickpeas (with liquid), chopped chard stems. Simmer 12 min.
Add chard leaves, cook 3 min. Off heat, squeeze lemon, big drizzle of olive oil.
